name: SAM (Segment Anything Model)

auth.notes: Local inference; model weights downloaded from Meta

performance.notes: GPU required for real-time use; image encoding ~20ms, mask decoding <5ms per prompt

agent_readiness.notes: Meta's foundation model for image segmentation. Prompt-based: click a point, draw a box, or provide text to segment any object. Three model sizes: ViT-H (2.5GB), ViT-L (1.2GB), ViT-B (375MB). SamPredictor for interactive segmentation, SamAutomaticMaskGenerator for everything-segmentation. Works with Grounded DINO for text-prompted detection + segmentation. SAM 2 extends to video segmentation. Supervision library integrates SAM for COCO-format annotations.

gotchas: GPU required for practical speed (CPU is 10-100x slower)
Amorphous or transparent objects difficult to segment
SAM 1 is image-only; SAM 2 needed for video
best_when: Interactive image segmentation for labeling and annotation tools
Combined with Grounded DINO for text-prompted detection + segmentation
Data annotation pipelines for computer vision datasets
avoid_when: Real-time video segmentation (use SAM 2 or lighter models)
Very small objects or fine-detail segmentation
summary: Meta's Segment Anything Model (SAM) is a foundation model for zero-shot image segmentation. Prompt with points, boxes, or text to segment any object. Three model sizes (ViT-H/L/B). SamPredictor for interactive use, SamAutomaticMaskGenerator for full-image segmentation. Pairs with Grounded DINO for text-prompted detection + segmentation. SAM 2 extends to video. Essential for annotation pipelines and zero-shot CV applications.
